item.title: eInk Radiator - #2: Basic Image Sources
item.description: The three basic image sources for the eInk Radiator project: Blank, image, and text

item.title: eInk Radiator - #1: Display
item.description: I describe the display component of the eInk Radiator project.

item.title: Portfolio
item.description:
This is a collection of examples of my work in software. I consider it to be a good representation of both my development abilities as well as my use of software development practices like CI/CD and test automation.

Professional work

The following have been developed to support the ISV partners during my time at both Pivotal and VMware. Much of this work is closed source and proprietary, but the following is representative of my open source work:

item.title: Cattle Cluster
item.description: When my cluster went down and how I didn’t fix it.

item.title: eInk Radiator - #0: Design
item.description: The start of a new series on the eInk Radiator project, a Raspberry PI-based eInk display.

item.title: Continuous Thing-Doer
item.description: I describe the way that I’ve deployed Concourse to my cluster.

item.title: Summoning Ghosts
item.description: Where I describe the process of deploying this blog software.

item.description: The challenges of getting the M.2 drives working and the dumb reason why.

item.description: Getting the firmware up to date on the hardware for the home lab.

item.title: Home Lab Build - #0 Current State
item.description: The history and current state of my home lab, and why it’s time to change it.

item.title: About Pete Wall
item.description:
I am a software engineer who loves to work with people to make great things. I have experience at small companies creating web apps and microprocessor firmware; at medium companies creating VR experiences and IRB-approved research projects; and at large enterprises creating robust and reliable software.

I love open source: Using great tools is cool. Creating great tools for others is better! Whether at work, or on personal projects, I want to contribute quality code to benefit everyone.
